title_id: Bagian 14 — Surat kepada Raja Siprus: Keadilan Umat Islam dan Seruan kepada Raja
content_id:
Pesan ini adalah penyembahan Allah semata tanpa sekutu, penyerahan yang tulus kepada Allah dalam semua bidang agama. Allah memerintahkan Rasul terakhir-Nya ﷺ untuk mengarahkan shalatnya dan hajinya menuju Rumah suci-Nya yang dibangun oleh bapak para nabi dan imam para penganut tauhid yang sejati, Ibrahim. Allah juga memerintahkan agar umat Rasul terakhir menjadi umat yang adil, tidak melebih-lebihkan kedudukan para nabi sehingga menjadikan mereka setara dengan Pencipta mereka atau memberikan kepada mereka atribut-atribut yang hanya dimiliki oleh-Nya. Sama-sama umat ini tidak mengabaikan mereka dengan cara serupa dengan mereka yang meremehkan, tidak mematuhi, dan kemudian menyakiti mereka. Sebaliknya, anggota-anggota umat ini menghormati, membantu, dan percaya kepada pesan para nabi. Mereka hanya menyembah Allah, menempatkan kepercayaan dan berlindung hanya kepada-Nya sebagai orang-orang yang beriman tauhid yang tulus dan sejati.

Umat Muhammad ﷺ juga adil dalam syariat mereka. Mereka menyatakan ketaatan mereka kepada perintah-perintah dan larangan-larangan Allah. Siapa pun selain para nabi dan rasul Allah, tidak memiliki mandat untuk mengubah agama Allah, juga tidak memiliki kekuasaan untuk berinovasi dalam agama apa yang tidak Dia izinkan. Mandat para nabi adalah menyampaikan pesan Allah. Hanya kepada-Nya milik makhluk-makhluk dan kewenangan. Jika tidak ada yang mampu menciptakan maka tidak ada yang harus memerintah kecuali Dia. "Menetapkan hukum itu hanyalah hak Allah. Dia menerangkan yang sebenarnya dan Dia Pemberi keputusan yang paling baik." (12:40).

Umat ini berada pada jalan tengah dalam masalah-masalah kesucian dan kenajisan, dalam masalah-masalah halal dan haram, dan juga dalam masalah-masalah akhlak. Mereka tidak hanya mengandalkan kekasaran atau kelembutan saja. Sebaliknya, mereka memperlakukan musuh-musuh Allah dengan ketegasan dan mereka yang bergantung kepada-Nya dengan kasih sayang, dan mereka berbicara tentang al-Masih sebagaimana Tuhan dan para murid al-Masih mengatakan tentangnya. Mereka tidak mengikuti inovasi-inovasi mereka yang melampaui batas kekaguman terhadapnya, juga tidak mereka yang mengabaikannya sepenuhnya. Yang mendorong keinginanku untuk menulis kepada kepala bangsa dan agamanya adalah bahwa sampai kepadaku pengetahuan tentang kehormatannya, minat yang tulus dalam agama, dan kecintaan terhadap ilmu. Aku juga mendapat perhatian terhadap kesan dan pujian Syaikh Abu al-Abbas al-Maqdisi, atas perilaku yang baik dan antusiasme Raja serta akhlak yang baik para ulamanya. Kami sungguh-sungguh adalah orang-orang yang dermawan, dan berharap agar Allah memberikan kepadamu pahala terbaik di dunia dan akhirat.
content_en: This message was the sole worship of Allaah without partners, the sincere submission to Allaah in all areas of the Deen, purifying the earth from the worship of idols and the religion of minor and major shirk. This occurred as idols were being worshipped in the lands of Sham, the lands of the sons of Israa'eel and of those who proclaim their Christianity. The last Messenger (saw) called all to believe in all of the revealed books of Allaah, i.e. the Torah, the Bible, the Psalms, and the Qur'aan and in His Prophets from Aadam to Muhammad (saw). Allaah says: "And they say, "Be Jews", or "Christians", and "you shall be guided". Say [to them, O Muhammad]: "Nay, but [ours is] the creed of Ibraaheem, who turned away from all that is false, and was not of those who ascribe divinity to any beside Allaah". Say: "We believe in Allaah and in that which has been bestowed from on high upon us, and that which has been bestowed upon Ibraaheem and Isma'eel and Ishaq and Ya'qoob and their descendants, and that which has been vouchsafed to Moosa and 'Eesaa and that which has been vouchsafed to all the [other] prophets by their Sustainer: we make no distinction between any of them. And it is unto Him that we surrender ourselves [in Islaam]". And if [others] come to believe in the way youbelieve, they will indeed find themselves on the right path; and if they turn away, it is but they who will be deeply in the wrong, and Allaah will suffice you against them, for He alone is All-Hearer the All-Knower". (2:135-7) Allaah has also commanded this prophet to call the creation to singling Him out with oneness and uniqueness through the establishment of justice. He says: "Say [O Muhammad], "O People of the Book, come unto that tenet which we and you hold in common: that we shall worship none but Allaah, and that we shall not associate partners with Him, and that we shall not take human beings for our lords beside Allaah". And if they turn away, then say, "Bear witness that we are Muslims". (3:64) Almighty Allaah also said, "And it is not given to mortal man that Allaah should speak unto him unless [it is] by revelation or from behind a veil". (42:51) "It is not conceivable that a human being unto whom Allaah had granted revelation, and sound judgement, and prophethood should thereafter have said unto people, "Worship none beside Allaah", but rather [did he exhort them], "Become men of Allaah by spreading knowledge of the divine writ, and by your own deep study [thereof]." And neither did he bid you to take the angels and the prophets for your lords: [for] would he bid you to deny the truth after you have surrendered yourselves unto Allaah" (3:79-80) Allaah has ordered His last Messenger (saw) to direct his prayer and pilgrimage towards His sacred house built by the father of the prophets and the imaam of the true monotheist believers, Ibraaheem. Allaah had also ordered that the nation of the last messenger be a just one, nor exaggerating the status of the prophets so as to make them equal to their Creator or bestowing on them features solely possessed by Him. Equally this person did not disregard them in a similar way to those who belittled, disobeyed and then harmed them. Instead, the members of this nation respected, aided and believed in the prophets' message. Moreover this nation had obeyed, followed, trusted, loved and extolled the prophets themselves. They had only worshipped Allaah, placing trust and taking refuge in Him alone as sincere and true monotheists. Muhammad's (saw) nation was also just in their sharee'ah. They proclaim their adherence to Allaah's commandments and prohibitions. They say that Allaah had prohibited the sons of Israa'eel from what He allowed earlier for Israa'eel himself, and He made lawful through the Messiah what was previously prohibited to the sons of Israa'eel. In both analogies Allaah orders and his servants hear and obey. Anyone else, apart from Allaah's prophets and messengers, possess no mandate to change the religion of Allaah, nor power to innovate in the religion what He did not allow. The prophets' mandate is to deliver the message of Allaah. To Him alone belongs the creations and authority. If none is able to create then none should rule but Him. "Judgement rests with Allaah alone - He has ordained that you should worship none but Him: this is the [one] ever-true faith; but most people know it not". (12:40) This nation is one the middle way in matters of purity and impurity, in matters of lawful and prohibited, and also in matters of morals. They have neither relied solely on harshness or on tenderness. Instead, they treated the enemies of Allaah with severity and those who rely on Him with mercy, and they speak of the Messiah that which the Lord and the disciples of the Messiah say of him. They neither follow the innovations of those who surpassed the limit of his admiration, nor those who had neglected him completely. What triggered my desire to write to the Chief of his people and religion, is that came to my knowledge of his esteem, genuine interest in religion, and love for knowledge.
content_ar:
يُركّز ابن تيمية في هذا الجزء على وصف الأمة المحمدية بأنها الأمة الوسط العادلة في منهجها وشريعتها: فهي لا تغلو في الأنبياء فتجعلهم شركاءَ لله في صفاته وحقوقه، ولا تجفو فيهم فتُهين مقامهم أو تُكذّب رسالتهم. وهي تُؤمن بالأنبياء جميعاً وتُطيع الله في جميع أوامره. ومن لم يكن نبياً فليس له أن يُشرّع في الدين ما لم يُشرّعه الله، لأن الحكم لله وحده: «إنِ الحُكْمُ إلَّا للهِ» (يوسف: 40).

وفي مقابل الغلو النصراني الذي رفع المسيح إلى مرتبة الألوهية، وفي مقابل الجفاء اليهودي الذي قتل الأنبياء وكذّب المرسلين، تسلك أمة محمد صلى الله عليه وسلم الوسطية: تُحبّ المسيح وتُعظّمه عبداً لله ورسولاً، وتُؤمن بما جاء به حقاً، وتُصرّح بما قاله هو وحوارييه الأولون من دون الإضافات التي أدخلها من بعدهم.

ثم يُفصح ابن تيمية عن الدافع الذي حمله على كتابة هذه الرسالة إلى ملك قبرص: بلغه عن الملك حبّ العلم والصدق في طلب الحق، وأبلغه شيخ أبو العباس المقدسي بحُسن أخلاق الملك وثناؤه على المسلمين. فرأى ابن تيمية أن من أجلّ ما يُهديه لمثله: دعوتُه إلى الحق بإيضاح أصول الدين وأسسه، وتنبيهه على ما الطريق إلى السعادة في الدنيا والآخرة.

تكشف هذه الفقرات عن ابن تيمية في أبهى صوره: عالمٌ رباني لا تشغله شدائد السجن عن الدعوة إلى الله وبيان الحق، يُخاطب الملك غير المسلم بالمحبة والنصح الصادق لا بالتهديد والتعالي، مؤمناً بأن الرسالة الكبرى للمسلم هي الإسهام في هداية الناس بكل ما أوتي من قدرة وعلم.
